root_text.an3.51:1.1: Atha kho dve brāhmaṇā jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.an3.51:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te brāhmaṇā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an3.51:1.3: “mayamassu, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā;
root_text.an3.51:1.5: Ovadatu no bhavaṁ gotamo, anusāsatu no bhavaṁ gotamo yaṁ amhākaṁ assa dīgharattaṁ hitāya sukhāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.51:2.1: “Taggha tumhe, brāhmaṇā, jiṇṇā vuddhā mahallakā addhagatā vayoanuppattā vīsavassasatikā jātiyā;
root_text.an3.51:2.3: Upanīyati kho ayaṁ, brāhmaṇā, loko jarāya byādhinā maraṇena.
root_text.an3.51:2.4: Evaṁ upanīyamāne kho, brāhmaṇā, loke jarāya byādhinā maraṇena, yo idha kāyena saṁyamo vācāya saṁyamo manasā saṁyamo, taṁ tassa petassa tāṇañca leṇañca dīpañca saraṇañca parāyaṇañcāti.

translation_text.an3.51:1.1: Then two old brahmins—elderly and senior, who were advanced in years and had reached the final stage of life, a hundred and twenty years old—went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an3.51:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.51:1.3: “We brahmins, worthy Gotama, are old, elderly and senior, we’re advanced in years and have reached the final stage of life; we’re a hundred and twenty years old.
translation_text.an3.51:1.4: And we haven’t done what is good and skillful, nor have we made a shelter from fear.
translation_text.an3.51:1.5: Advise us, worthy Gotama, instruct us! It will be for our lasting welfare and happiness.”
translation_text.an3.51:2.1: “Indeed, brahmins, you’re old, elderly and senior.
translation_text.an3.51:2.2: And you haven’t done what is good and skillful, nor have you made a shelter from fear.
translation_text.an3.51:2.3: This world is led onward by old age, sickness, and death.
translation_text.an3.51:2.4: But restraint here by way of body, speech, and mind is the shelter, asylum, island, refuge, and final haven for the departed.
translation_text.an3.51:3.1: This life, so very short, is led onward.
translation_text.an3.51:3.2: One led on to old age has no shelter.
translation_text.an3.51:3.4: do good deeds that bring you joy.
translation_text.an3.51:4.4: as the good deeds done while living.”
